what_it_does: Lists a batch allocation of six HTML preview files for the claude-ds design system, recording each file's project, relative path, and root directory.
observations: All entries share the same project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'
Every path is under the 'preview/' subdirectory and ends with '.html'
File names reference design-token documentation topics: spacing_scale, type_body, type_display, type_mono, type_scale, type_screen_title
The file contains no code symbols or content bodies
It appears to function as a batch manifest or ledger allocation entry
standouts: Tight focus on typography and spacing scale previews
All artifacts belong to a single design-system project root
No embedded code or symbol metadata
concerns: No timestamps, versions, or build-order hints
No explicit dependencies or relationships among the preview files
Paths are listed without verification of existence or generated status
notes: A batch ledger entry enumerating six claude-ds design-system preview HTML files covering typography and spacing scales.
questions: What does 'wave-002/alloc/batch-033' signify for processing order or grouping?
Are these preview files generated artifacts or hand-authored documentation?
What is the expected output of interpreting this batch?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the path suggests an interpretive allocation batch, and the content is a manifest of preview files to be processed.
intention_signals: It is trying to catalog a set of HTML preview artifacts so they can be interpreted or fused as a scoped batch within a build-prep pipeline.
contribution: Provides a scoped inventory of claude-ds preview HTML files, giving a fusion system a concrete list of design-system documentation artifacts to ingest.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON batch manifest listing six HTML preview pages for the claude-ds design system under preview/, covering spacing and typography scales.
intention_for_embedding: To allocate a set of design-system preview files for interpretive processing in wave-002 batch-033.
